---
package: "openpipeline"
version: "v4.2.0"
title: "delimit_fraction"
namespace: "Filter"
description: "Turns a column containing values between 0 and 1 into a boolean column based on thresholds"
type: "module"
toc: false
latest: "true"
subtitle: "openpipeline v4.2.0"
---

::: {.column-margin}

### Info

ID: `delimit_fraction`  
Namespace: `filter`

### Links

[{{< fa brands github >}} View source code](https://github.com/openpipelines-bio/openpipeline/tree/v4.2.0/src/filter/delimit_fraction)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Browse all versions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline)

[![](/images/viash-hub-logo.svg){.vh-icon} Get run instructions](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/filter/delimit_fraction)

:::



## Example commands

You can run the pipeline using `nextflow run`.

### View help

You can use `--help` as a parameter to get an overview of the possible parameters.

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -main-script target/nextflow/filter/delimit_fraction/main.nf \
  --help
```

### Run command

<details>
<summary>Example of `params.yaml`</summary>

Generate a `params.yaml` for this component on [Viash Hub](https://www.viash-hub.com/packages/openpipeline/v4.2.0/components/filter/delimit_fraction).

</details>

Run locally with:

```bash
nextflow run openpipelines-bio/openpipeline \
  -r v4.2.0 -latest \
  -profile docker \
  -main-script target/nextflow/filter/delimit_fraction/main.nf \
  -params-file params.yaml
```

:::{.callout-note}
Replace `-profile docker` with `-profile podman` or `-profile singularity` depending on the desired backend.
:::

Running on Seqera or through Viash Hub is explained in the [guides](/guides/index.qmd).

## Argument groups

### Inputs

|Name                    |Description                                                                                                |Attributes                                              |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--input`               |Input h5mu file                                                                                            |`file`, required, example: `"input.h5mu"`               |
|`--modality`            |Which modality from the input MuData file to process.                                                      |`string`, default: `"rna"`                              |
|`--obs_fraction_column` |Name of column from .var dataframe selecting a column that contains floating point values between 0 and 1. |`string`, required, example: `"fraction_mitochondrial"` |


### Outputs

|Name                   |Description                                                                                                    |Attributes                       |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--output`             |Output h5mu file.                                                                                              |`file`, example: `"output.h5mu"` |
|`--obs_name_filter`    |In which .obs slot to store a boolean array corresponding to which observations should be removed.             |`string`, required               |
|`--output_compression` |Compression format to use for the output AnnData and/or Mudata H5 files. By default no compression is applied. |`string`, example: `"gzip"`      |


### Arguments

|Name             |Description                                                      |Attributes             |
|:----------|:--------------------------------------------------|:--------------------|
|`--min_fraction` |Min fraction for an observation to be retained (True in output). |`double`, default: `0` |
|`--max_fraction` |Max fraction for an observation to be retained (True in output). |`double`, default: `1` |

## Authors


  * Dries Schaumont [{{< fa brands github >}}](https://github.com/DriesSchaumont) [{{< fa brands orcid >}}](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-4389-0440) [{{< fa brands linkedin >}}](https://linkedin.com/in/dries-schaumont) (author, maintainer)
